text: <!channel> :red_circle: CDN 502 error rate just spiked to 8.4%. PagerDuty INC-804 triggered. Check CDN-ALERTS channel.
<@U02CHARLIE> you're on-call for CDN. INC-804 is high urgency.
Acknowledging INC-804. Looking at Sentry now.
Sentinel shows CacheKeyMismatchError on all POPs. Cache hit ratio dropped from 92% to 0.3%. Checking recent deploys.
PR #4381 was deployed at 02:00 UTC. Cache key normalization for tiered distribution.
That's it. The cache key change skips normalization for authenticated requests. They're all bypassing cache and hitting origin. Opening revert now.
Revert PR #4382 is up. Need two approvals. <@U02CHARLIE> <@U05DIANA>
Approved and merged. Cache purge starting now.
Cache purge initiated on all POPs. ETA ~30s for invalidation.
Error rate dropping. Now at 2.1% and falling. Cache hit ratio recovering: 67%.
Error rate back to baseline (0.12%). Resolving INC-804 and INC-805.
:large_green_circle: CDN 502 error rate normalized. Total impact: 47 minutes. ~32k users affected. Postmortem scheduled tomorrow 10am.

Root cause: PR #4381 introduced a cache key normalization bug. Authenticated requests were not normalized → always missed cache → origin pool overwhelmed → 502 errors cascade.

Key issues: 1) No canary deployment for config changes 2) No automated cache hit ratio check 3) Review missed the edge case.

Postmortem action items drafted as PR #4383 and #4384.
Agreed on all points. I'll own the canary deployment PR. The cache key logic needs better test coverage too.
Adding monitoring: we need a dashboard for cache hit ratio per-POP with PagerDuty integration under 50%.
Great analysis. Let's also add an automated rollback trigger when 502 rate exceeds 2% within 3 minutes of any CDN deploy.
